topic: If Clauses / Conditionals Part 1 — Zero & First Conditional
keyPointsSummary: Zero conditional describes general facts/scientific truths that are always true
First conditional describes a real, likely future situation and its result
Both clauses can switch order without changing meaning (comma only needed when 'if' clause comes first)
Do not use 'will' in the if-clause

quickQuiz.hindi: अगर पानी 100 डिग्री तक गर्म होता है, तो वह उबलता है।
quickQuiz.answer: If water heats to 100 degrees, it boils.

quickQuiz.hindi: अगर तुम बर्फ को गर्म करते हो, तो वह पिघल जाती है।
quickQuiz.answer: If you heat ice, it melts.

quickQuiz.hindi: अगर मुझे समय मिला, तो मैं आऊँगा।
quickQuiz.answer: If I get time, I will come.

quickQuiz.hindi: अगर बारिश हुई, तो हम घर पर रहेंगे।
quickQuiz.answer: If it rains, we will stay home.

quickQuiz.hindi: अगर तुम मेहनत करोगे, तो सफल होगे।
quickQuiz.answer: If you work hard, you will succeed.

quickQuiz.hindi: अगर वह जल्दी आया, तो हम शुरू करेंगे।
quickQuiz.answer: If he comes early, we will start.

quickQuiz.hindi: अगर सूरज निकलता है, तो दिन गर्म होता है।
quickQuiz.answer: If the sun comes out, the day is warm.

quickQuiz.hindi: अगर तुम यह खाओगे, तो तुम्हें अच्छा लगेगा।
quickQuiz.answer: If you eat this, you will feel good.

quickQuiz.hindi: अगर तुम्हें मदद चाहिए, तो मुझे बताओ।
quickQuiz.answer: If you need help, tell me.

quickQuiz.hindi: अगर वह नहीं आया, तो हम शुरू करेंगे।
quickQuiz.answer: If he doesn't come, we will start.

mustRememberRules: Zero conditional describes general facts/scientific truths that are always true
First conditional describes a real, likely future situation and its result
Both clauses can switch order without changing meaning (comma only needed when 'if' clause comes first)
Do not use 'will' in the if-clause
